title: forsteronia umbellata
intro: Forsteronia umbellata, also known as the Guanacurito or Wild Canelo, is a plant species native to South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Forsteronia umbellata include its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, as well as its use as an antiseptic and antispasmodic. In herbal preparations, Forsteronia umbellata is often used in teas, infusions, and extracts, and can also be found in some commercial remedies for its medicinal properties.
